localizedDescriptions.en: Interaction, contact or care event between a subject of care and healthcare provider(s).
A test to quantify an individual's ability to resolve small objects as a measure of the clarity or sharpness of vision.
Default, unspecified point in time or interval event which may be explicitly defined in a template or at run-time.
The eye which is being examined, or both eyes in case of a binocular test.
The left eye was examined.
The right eye was examined.
Both eyes were examined simultaneously, typically to assess binocular visual acuity.
Only one of the contained elements should be used per visual acuity test.
Measures of visual acuity that are qualitative, rather than quantitative.

annotations.comment: Light Projection testing is not a VA test, use OBSERVATION.light_projection_test.v0 instead.

localizedDescriptions.en: The individual perceived hand movements.
The individual correctly counted fingers.
The individual correnctly identified the absence or presence of light.
The individual was unable to identify the absence or presence of light.

Snellen Ratio in meters, where the numerator is the (equivalent or real) testing distance in meters and the denominator is the distance in meters at which the spatial pattern would be separated by a visual angle of one minute of arc. This means the denominator is the distance in meters at which a person with 6/6 visual acuity could still read the line.

annotations.comment:
Often used with the standard of 6 meters (6/x), which may  or may not be the real testing distance. 
When M Notation is used, the nominator is also the real testing distance.

localizedDescriptions.en: Base-10 Logarithm of the minimum angle of resolution.

annotations.comment: This notation used in the majority of scientific publications. When users enter visual acuity test results using decimal numbers, such as 0.5,  it needs to be ensured that the intended notation (decimal, logMAR or others) is known to both the user and the software, and the appropriate element is used.

localizedDescriptions.en: Notation commonly used in Europe, defined as the reciprocal of the minimum agle of resolution in arc minutes.

annotations.comment: Can be calculating by dividing the snellen ratio's numerator by its denominator. When users enter visual acuity test results using decimal numbers, such as 0.5,  it needs to be ensured that the intended notation (decimal, logMAR or others) is known to both the user and the software, and the appropriate element is used.

localizedDescriptions.en: N font size of the smallest recognized line or text.

annotations.comment: Strictly not a unit of visual acuity but a measure of symbol size commonly used in near vision testing.  A known testing distance is required to estimate the visual angle, 30-40cm is typical for reading distances.

localizedDescriptions.en: The number of optotypes correctly identified by the patient on a logMAR/ETDRS Chart, including those that are presumed to be readable due to being larger than the smallest line that was read entirely. This includes letters presumed readable at 1m distance.

annotations.comment:
Commonly used in study settings. This score describes either the number of optotypes identified (letters read): 
1. Out of 30 at 1m distance. 
Or
2. Out of 70 at 4m distance. In which case the 30 letters from the 1m distance are added to the result as they are presumed readable.

An ETDRS letter score of 85 is equivalent to 20/20, 1.0 decimal, 0.0 logMAR. 
Conversion of this notation requires understanding of testing procedures and intended meaning.

localizedDescriptions.en: Jaeger font size of the smallest recognized line or text.

annotations.comment: The Jaeger font sizes denoted by the same number are known to vary from card to card.

localizedDescriptions.en: Base-10 Logarithm of the Minimum Angle of resolution.

children.name: Letters missed in or read in addition to scored line
children.localizedName: Letters missed in or read in addition to scored line

localizedNames.en: Letters missed in or read in addition to scored line

localizedDescriptions.en: Negative:  Number of Letters missed in the smallest line that was recorded as being successfully read. Positive: Letters correctly identified in the largest line that was not successfully read. Coded values refer to concept of "partially" read line.

annotations.comment: Line-based scoring means lines are scored in their entirety based on whether a certain threshhold (typically 50 or 60%) of their letters were read corretly. Based on this scoring method, it is correct to note the smallest line where that threshold was reached as the visual acuity grade and that is what is expected based on ISO standards. Some persons and institutions additionally record the number of missed letters in the smallest scored line (negative count), the fact that there were missed letters in the smallest scored line (partial) or the number or letters read beyond the smallest scored line  (positive count).

children.localizedName: Letters missed in or read in addition to scored line

localizedNames.en: Letters missed in or read in addition to scored line

localizedDescriptions.en: Negative:  Number of Letters missed in the smallest line that was recorded as being successfully read. Positive: Letters correctly identified in the largest line that was not successfully read. Coded values refer to concept of "partially" read line.

children.localizedName: Letters missed in or read in addition to scored line

localizedNames.en: Letters missed in or read in addition to scored line

localizedDescriptions.en: Negative:  Number of Letters missed in the smallest line that was recorded as being successfully read. Positive: Letters correctly identified in the largest line that was not successfully read. Coded values refer to concept of "partially" read line.

Some symbols were not read correctly in the smallest line that was scored as being successully read, which is not unusual as the line being scored as correct requires 50 or 60% of symbols being identified correctly. Not actually part of line-based scoring, and a bit controversial but commonly used anyway.
An ordinal measure of testing distance

annotations.comment: Ideally to be used in conjunction with the quantitative element for testing distance.

localizedDescriptions.en: The test was performed at near distance, which typically refers to a distance below 50cm. There is no generally agreed cutoff.
The test was performed at intermediate distance, which typically refers to distances between 50cm and 2 Meters. There are no generally agreed cutoffs.
The test was performed at far distance, which typically refers to distances at or above 4 meters, without a generally accepted cutoff.
The distance between the tested eye and the apparent location of the stimulus.

annotations.comment: The stimulus may be a virtual image.

localizedDescriptions.en: A general categorization of testing methods.
A printed chart or card was used.
An electronic display was used.
A chart projector was used.
Physical objects other than Charts or Cards were used,
A chart presented as a virtual image inside a device, such as an autorefractor.
The specific  chart or card method used to measure visual acuity.
A logMAR chart was used.
A Snellen chart was used.
An ETDRS chart was used.
An ETDRS original series chart 1 was used.
An ETDRS original series chart 2 was used.
An ETDRS original series chart R was used.
An ETDRS revised series chart 1 was used.
An ETDRS revised series chart 2 was used.
An ETDRS revised series chart 3 was used.
A Golovin-Sitsev table was used.
A monoyer visual acuity chart was used.
Cardiff acuity cards were used.
Keeler acuity cards were used.
Teller acuity cards were used.
Berkeley rudimentary vision test was used. Note that white field projection or black/white discrimination is not a measure of VA.
A near card with segments of text that are read by the individual was used.
LEA gratings, a type of preferential looking test was used.
Teller acuity cards were used.
The cardiff acuity test was used.
The allen picture test was used.
The sheridian letter test was ued.
Cambridge crowding cards were used.
A MNREAD acuity chart was used.
A RADNER reading chart was used.
The freiburg visual acuity test was used.
The sheridan gardiner test was used.
The Sonksen silver test was used

An instrument, apparatus, implant, material or similar, used in the provision of healthcare. In this context, a medical device includes a broad range of devices which act through a variety of physical, mechanical, thermal or similar means but specifically excludes devices which act through medicinal means such as pharmacological, metabolic or immunological methods.  The scope is inclusive of disposable devices as well as durable or persisting devices that require tracking, maintenance activities or regular calibration, recognising that each type of device has specific data recording requirements.
Identification of the medical device, preferably by a common name, a formal fully descriptive name or, if required, by class or category of device.

annotations.comment: This data element will capture the term, phrase or category used in clinical practice. For example: <brand name><machine> (XYZ Audiometer); <size> <brand name> <intravenous catheter> (14G Jelco IV catheter); or <brand name/type> <implant>. Coding with a terminology is desirable, where possible, although this may be local and depending on local supplies available.

localizedDescriptions.en: Name of manufacturer.
The type of visual acuity chart symbol used to assess visual acuity.
Picture optotypes were used.
Orientation-Type optotypes were used.
Letter optotypes were used.
Lea Symbol  optotypes were used.
Kay Picture  optotypes were used.
Allen Picture  optotypes were used.
Auckland  optotypes were used.
Amsterdam Picture  optotypes were used.
Landolt C optotypes were used.
Tumbling E optotypes were used.
Number optotypes were used.
Precision Vision Number optotypes were used.
Lea Number optotypes were used.
Sloan Letter optotypes were used.
Cyrillic Letter optotypes were used.
Snellen Letter optotypes were used.
HOTV Letter optotypes were used.
LRVC Letter optotypes were used.
British Letter (2003) optotypes were used.
European-wide letter optotypes were used.
Modifications of how optotypes are displayed

annotations.comment: Differentiating crowded from uncrowded optotypes is especially relevant in children and those with a suspicion of Amblyopia.

localizedDescriptions.en: A single optotype at a time was displayed.
Crowded optotypes were displayed.
A whole line of optotypes of the same size were displayed at the same time.
Uncrowded optotypes were displayed.
A note indicating that the addition of another type of aid did not result in a better test result.

annotations.comment: Used where a determination of the visual acuity with the additional aid was not deemed sufficiently important to warrant being performed.

localizedDescriptions.en: No improvement  beyond the recorded result was noted after addition of a pinhole.

list.label: No Improvement with subjective refraction-based correction

localizedLabels.en: No Improvement with subjective refraction-based correction

localizedDescriptions.en: No improvement beyond the recorded result was noted using corrective lenses based on subjective refraction.
No improvement beyond the recorded result was noted after addition of corrective lenses based on an autorefraction test.
No improvement beyond the recorded result was noted after addition of corrective lenses based on retinoscopy.
No improvement beyond the recorded result was noted after the addition of corrective glasses.

list.label: No improvement with soft contact lens

localizedLabels.en: No improvement with soft contact lens

localizedDescriptions.en: No improvement beyond the recorded result was noted after the addition of a soft contact lens.

list.label: No improvement with rigid contact lens

localizedLabels.en: No improvement with rigid contact lens

localizedDescriptions.en: No improvement beyond the recorded result was noted after the addition of a rigid or hard contact lens
Narrative comment about the visual acuity test.

annotations.comment: It is a common but controversial practice to comment "partial" where the last line was not read entirely even in line-based scoring. This is covered by the "Letters missed or read in addition to scored line" element.

localizedDescriptions.en: Interpretation of the results.

annotations.comment: Not to be used for information about how the test was performed or patient circumstances affecting result.

localizedDescriptions.en: Classification of the test and its result as "best" corrected or "best" recorded visual acuity, or both.

annotations.comment: There is significant variation to how these terms are definied in different settings.  These classifiers do not contain information on the actual correction used, which can be recorded within the right/left aid position clusters.

localizedDescriptions.en: This result is classified as a Best Corrected Visual Acuity.
This result is classified as a Best Recorded Visual Acuity.
Positive statement to record that a physical examination or clinical test was not performed.
Statement to explicity record that the examination was not performed.

annotations.comment: Record as True if the examination was not performed.

localizedDescriptions.en: Reason for the 'not done' statement.

annotations.comment: For example: patient factors, equipment factors, time constraints.

localizedDescriptions.en: A cluster describing what type of visual aid was used during the test, and which eye it was positioned in front of.

annotations.comment: Where multiple devices are positioned in front of the same eye, this cluster ist expected to be used once for each. Example: Pinhole occluder and phoropter lenses being used at the same time.

localizedDescriptions.en: The eye in front of which the aid device or correction, such as an arrangement of trial lenses, own glasses, contact lenses, pinhole occluders are positioned.

annotations.comment: For monocular tests where the partner eye is fully occluded, this can be "The eye being tested". There may be exceptions, such as when the eye that is not being tested explicitely has a fogging lens in front of it to blur, but not block all vision. In binocular tests, this element is expected to occur once with "right eye" and once with "left eye" for each aid placed in front of the eyes.

localizedDescriptions.en: The left eye was examined.
The right eye was examined.
The aid was in front of the eye being tested. For monocular test where the other eye is occluded, this is the default value.
The type of lens or object that is positioned in front of one of the eyes.

annotations.comment:
Which eye it is in front of depends on the value of the "In front of which eye" element.
Note that this is not exclusive of devices that are not corrective, such as defocus or fogging lenses.

localizedDescriptions.en: A correction with powers based on an autorefraction test.
A correction with powers based on a subjective refraction.
Glasses were used as an aid.
A rigid contact lens correciton was used. Sometimes called hard contact lens.
A soft contact lens.
A Correction with values based on retinoscopy.
Lenses without a specific origin of the values were used.
A Lens used to intentionally blur the vision in that eye.
A pinhole occluder was used which is a device with small holes that act as apertures. Sometimes used together with lenses.
A correction with values based on subjective overrefraction was used.
There is explicitely nothing in front of the eye during the visual acuity testing.
A note of whether the correction used was based on a measurement of the refractive error in a cycloplegic state.
The correction used was based on a measurement of refractive error in a cycloplegic state.
The correction used was based on a measurement of refractive error in a non-cycloplegic state.
The refractive power that is equal in all meridians, used as the base correction upon which cylinder and axis are applied.
The refractive power added to the spherical correction at the meridian 90° from the cylinder axis to compensate for astigmatism.
The orientation of the cylinder, defining the meridian of zero power, with maximum cylindrical power occurring 90° from this position.
The additional spherical lens power for near vision.
